revision.comment: Imported from wikipedia at 2025-05-11 01:43:06 on [[Template:Climate chart/doc]]
revision.text:
Used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures in C and precipitation in milimiters. ==Usage== The first parameter appears in the heading. The following 36 parameters are the average minimum temperature, average maximum temperature and average precipitation for each month. You can use the named arguments float and clear to control the positioning of the chart. ==Examples== {{climate chart|[[Buenos Aires]] |17|29|79 |17|28|71 |16|26|109 |12|22|89 |8|18|76 |5|14|61 |6|14|56 |6|16|61 |8|18|79 |10|21|86 |13|24|84 |16|28|99 |float=left }} 

{{climate chart|[[Buenos Aires]]

|16|28|99
|float=left
}}

Used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures in C and precipitation in milimiters. ==Usage== ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. This is the default state - no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ==Usage== ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. More information on the interpretation of the chart can be found [[Template:Climate chart/info|here]]. ===Units=== In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state - no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' ==Usage== ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. {{Documentation subpage}} {{TOCRight}} {{warning|'''Before making any changes to the template''', please first try and compare your changes in the test template: [[Template:Infobox Climate chart/sandbox]]. Thank you.}} ==Purpose== Used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. More information on the interpretation of the chart can be found [[Template:Climate chart/info|here]]. ===Units=== In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state - no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' ==Usage== ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with 3 columns for minimum temperature, maximum temperature and precipitation. ;float :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source :Supply the source of your data ==Examples== ===Float: right=== {{climate chart|[[Amsterdam]] |-7|-1|40 |-6|0|28 |-2|5|29 |4|11|26 |10|18|62 |15|24|73 |18|26|73 |17|25|89 |13|21|67 |7|14|52 |2|7|60 |-4|2|51 |float=right |clear=both }}

{{Documentation subpage}} {{warning|Before making any changes to the template, please first try and compare your changes in the test template: [[Template:Infobox Climate chart/sandbox]]. Thank you.}} Climate charts provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the amount of [[precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in millimeters or liters per square meter. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin red line is 0 °C, the point of freezing, for orientation. {{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-7|-2|30 |-7|-1|34 |-3|4|54 |1|9|68 |7|16|97 |11|20|123 |12|22|116 |11|20|118 |8|15|100 |4|9|116 |-2|3|107 |-6|0|84 |float=right }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cuzco|Cuzco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{-}} As we can see from the chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with hot summers and freezing winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies in the heart of the tropics, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cuzco also lies near the equator, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cuzco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August. === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with 3 columns for minimum temperature, maximum temperature and precipitation. ;float :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source :Supply the source of your data === Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' === Examples === ==== Float: right ==== {{ infobox climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=both }}

In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). [[Category:Weather templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Exclude in print]] [[ar:قالب:خارطة مناخ]] [[cs:Šablona:Klimagram]] [[es:Plantilla:Climate chart/doc]] [[fa:الگو:نمودار آب و هوا]] [[fr:Modèle:Diagramme climatique]] [[ja:Template:Climate chart]] [[ja:Template:Climate chart/info]] [[ko:틀:기후 도표]] [[nl:Sjabloon:Klimatogram]] [[pt:Predefinição:Grafo Clima]] [[pt:Predefinição:Grafo Clima/info]] [[tr:Şablon:İklim diyagramı]] [[ru:Шаблон:Климатограмма]] [[zh:Template:Climate chart]] [[zh:Template:Climate chart/info]]

{{Documentation subpage}} {{warning|Before making any changes to the template, please first try and compare your changes in the test template: [[Template:Infobox Climate chart/sandbox]]. Thank you.}} Climate charts provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the amount of [[precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in millimeters or liters per square meter. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin red line is 0 °C, the point of freezing, for orientation. {{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-7|-2|30 |-7|-1|34 |-3|4|54 |1|9|68 |7|16|97 |11|20|123 |12|22|116 |11|20|118 |8|15|100 |4|9|116 |-2|3|107 |-6|0|84 |float=right }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cuzco|Cuzco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{-}} As we can see from the chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with hot summers and freezing winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies in the heart of the tropics, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cuzco also lies near the equator, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cuzco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August. === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with 3 columns for minimum temperature, maximum temperature and precipitation. ;float :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source :Supply the source of your data === Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' === Examples === ==== Float: right ==== {{ infobox climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=both }}

{{Documentation subpage}} {{warning|Before making any changes to the template, please first try and compare your changes in the test template: [[Template:Infobox Climate chart/sandbox]]. Thank you.}} Climate charts provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === {{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}} === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with 3 columns for minimum temperature, maximum temperature and precipitation. ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data === Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' === Examples === ==== Float: right ==== {{ infobox climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=both }}

{{Documentation subpage}} {{warning|Before making any changes to the template, please first try and compare your changes in the test template: [[Template:Climate chart/sandbox]]. Thank you.}} Climate charts provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === {{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}} === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for minimum temperature, middle column for maximum temperature and right column for precipitation. ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data ====Empty syntax====

=== Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). {{Documentation subpage}} {{warning|Before making any changes to the template, please first try and compare your changes in the test template: [[Template:Climate chart/sandbox]]. Thank you.}} Climate charts provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === {{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}} === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for minimum temperature, middle column for maximum temperature and right column for precipitation. ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data ====Empty syntax==== 
{{climate chart
| 
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
|float  = 
|clear  = 
|units  = 
|source = 
}}
=== Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' === Examples === ==== Float: right ==== {{climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=both }}

}}

Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for minimum temperature, middle column for maximum temperature and right column for precipitation. ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data ====Empty syntax==== 
{{climate chart
| 
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
|float  = 
|clear  = 
|units  = 
|source = 
}}
=== Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' === Examples === ==== Float: right ==== {{climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=both }}

revision.comment: /* Usage */ it was never intended for extreme temperatures (the original wording said somewhat ambiguously "average maximum" and "average minimum", which was meant to mean "average high" and "average low")
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{warning|Before making any changes to the template, please first try your changes in the [[Template:{{BASEPAGENAME}}/sandbox|sandbox]] and compare them on the [[Template:{{BASEPAGENAME}}/testcases|test cases]] page. This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === {{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}} === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for average low temperature, middle column for average high temperature and right column for precipitation. ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data ====Empty syntax====

| | |

| | |

{{Documentation subpage}} {{Use template sandbox testcases request}} Climate charts provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === {{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}} === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for average low temperature, middle column for average high temperature and right column for precipitation. ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data ====Empty syntax====

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{Use template sandbox testcases request}} Climate charts provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === {{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}} === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for average low temperature, middle column for average high temperature and right column for precipitation. ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data ====Empty syntax==== 
{{climate chart
| 
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
|float  = 
|clear  = 
|units  = 
|source = 
}}
=== Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' === Examples === ==== Float: right ==== {{climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=both }}

|1.9|6.2|74.9
|float=right
|clear=both
}}
{{clear}} ==== Float: left ==== {{climate chart | [[Bilbao]] |6|13|15 |6|14|15 |7|16|15 |8|17|17 |11|19|16 |14|22|12 |16|25|11 |16|25|11 |14|24|11 |12|21|15 |9|17|15 |7|14|14 |float=left |source=[http://www.bbc.co.uk/weather/ BBC Weather] }}

{{Documentation subpage}} {{Use template sandbox testcases request}} Climate charts provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === {{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}} === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for average low temperature, middle column for average high temperature and right column for precipitation. ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data ====Empty syntax==== 
{{climate chart
| 
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
|float  = 
|clear  = 
|units  = 
|source = 
}}
=== Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' === Examples === ==== Float: right ==== {{climate chart |[봉화]] |-10.3| 3.2|21.1 |-8.2|6|28.4| |-3.2|10.9|53.1 |1.8|18.4|75.3 |7.6|23.1|105.7 |13.2|26.4|157.4 |18.3|28|296.5 |18.2|28.6|242 |12.4|24.7|148.7 |4|19.7|36.5 |-2.6|12.2|34.6 |-8.3|5.6|18.6 |float=right |clear=right }} ==== Float: right ==== {{climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=right }} 

{{climate chart
| [[Amsterdam]]
|0.5|5.4|62.1
|0.2|6.0|43.4

}}

{{Documentation subpage}} {{Use template sandbox testcases request}} This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === {{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}} === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2-37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for average low temperature, middle column for average high temperature and right column for precipitation. ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data ====Empty syntax==== 
{{climate chart
| 
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
|float  = 
|clear  = 
|units  = 
|source = 
}}
=== Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' === Examples === ==== Float: right ==== {{climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=right }}

{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox | | [[Category:Weather templates]] [[Category:Chart, diagram and graph formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Exclude in print]] }} [[es:Plantilla:Climate chart]] [[fr:Modèle:Diagramme climatique]] [[nl:Sjabloon:Klimatogram]] [[no:Klimakart/dok]]

{{Documentation subpage}} {{Use template sandbox testcases request}} This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === {{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}} === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2–37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for average low temperature, middle column for average high temperature and right column for precipitation. ;maxprecip= :Supply highest monthly precipitation number if the blue precipitation bars pop out of the chart (happens if any of the monthly numbers is greater than about 750 mm (30 in.)) ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data ====Empty syntax==== 
{{climate chart
| 
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
|maxprecip = 
|float     = 
|clear     = 
|units     = 
|source    = 
}}
=== Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' === Examples === ==== Float: right ==== {{climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=right }}

=== Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). revision.comment: Since <tt> is obsolete in [[HTML5]], replace it with <kbd> – see [[HTML element#tt]], [[Wikipedia:HTML5]], and [[mw:Help:Extension:Linter/obsolete-tag]]. using [[Project:AWB|AWB]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{Use template sandbox testcases request}} This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === {{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}} === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2–37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for average low temperature, middle column for average high temperature and right column for precipitation. ;maxprecip= :Supply highest monthly precipitation number if the blue precipitation bars pop out of the chart (happens if any of the monthly numbers is greater than about 750 mm (30 in.)) ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data ====Empty syntax==== 
{{climate chart
| 
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
|maxprecip = 
|float     = 
|clear     = 
|units     = 
|source    = 
}}
=== Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' === Examples === ==== Float: right ==== {{climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=right }}

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{Use template sandbox testcases request}} This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures. === How to read a climate chart === {{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}} === Usage === ;argument 1 :Appears in the heading ;arguments 2–37 :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for average low temperature, middle column for average high temperature and right column for precipitation. ;maxprecip= :Supply highest monthly precipitation number if the blue precipitation bars pop out of the chart (happens if any of the monthly numbers is greater than about 750 mm (30 in.)) ;float= :''left'', ''right'' or ''none''. ;clear= :''left'', ''right'', ''both'' or ''none''. ;source= :Supply the source of your data ====Empty syntax==== 
{{climate chart
| 
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
|maxprecip = 
|float     = 
|clear     = 
|units     = 
|source    = 
}}
=== Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.'' === Examples === ==== Float: right ==== {{climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=right }} {{climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=right }} {{clear}} ==== Float: left ==== {{climate chart | [[Bilbao]] |6|13|15 |6|14|15 |7|16|15 |8|17|17 |11|19|16 |14|22|12 |16|25|11 |16|25|11 |14|24|11 |12|21|15 |9|17|15 |7|14|14 |float=left |source=[http://www.bbc.co.uk/weather/ BBC Weather] }} {{climate chart | [[Bilbao]] |6|13|15 |6|14|15 |7|16|15 |8|17|17 |11|19|16 |14|22|12 |16|25|11 |16|25|11 |14|24|11 |12|21|15 |9|17|15 |7|14|14 |float=left |source=[http://www.bbc.co.uk/weather/ BBC Weather] }} {{clear}} ==== Non-float ==== {{climate chart | [[Coober Pedy, South Australia|Coober Pedy]] |20.7|36.4|17.4 |20.8|35.7|22.8 |18.2|32.8|12.9 |14|27.6|6.5 |10.1|23.3|13.1 |7.2|18.8|14.4 |6.3|18.7|7.8 |7.4|20.7|9.2 |10.1|24.5|8.4 |13.6|28.9|14.9 |16.6|32.1|11.6 |19.1|34.6|17.5 }} {{climate chart | [[Coober Pedy, South Australia|Coober Pedy]] |20.7|36.4|17.4 |20.8|35.7|22.8 |18.2|32.8|12.9 |14|27.6|6.5 |10.1|23.3|13.1 |7.2|18.8|14.4 |6.3|18.7|7.8 |7.4|20.7|9.2 |10.1|24.5|8.4 |13.6|28.9|14.9 |16.6|32.1|11.6 |19.1|34.6|17.5 }} ==== Imperial units ==== {{climate chart | [[Anchorage, Alaska|Anchorage]] |9|22|.68 |12|26|.74 |18|34|.65 |29|44|.52 |39|55|.7 |47|62|1.06 |52|65|1.7 |49|63|2.93 |41|55|2.87 |28|40|2.09 |16|28|1.09 |11|24|1.075 |float=right |clear=right |units=imperial }} {{climate chart | [[Anchorage, Alaska|Anchorage]] |9|22|.68 |12|26|.74 |18|34|.65 |29|44|.52 |39|55|.7 |47|62|1.06 |52|65|1.7 |49|63|2.93 |41|55|2.87 |28|40|2.09 |16|28|1.09 |11|24|1.05 |float=right |clear=right |units=imperial }} ==== Large precipitation numbers (without re-scaling) ==== {{climate chart |[[Baguio City, Philippines]] |12 |22 |20 |13 |22 |20 |14 |24 |40 |15 |25 |100 |16 |24 |400 |16 |23 |430 |15 |21 |1070 |15 |21 |1160 |15 |21 |710 |15 |22 |380 |15 |23 |120 |13 |23 |50 |float=right |clear=right }} {{climate chart |[[Baguio City, Philippines]] |12 |22 |20 |13 |22 |20 |14 |24 |40 |15 |25 |100 |16 |24 |400 |16 |23 |430 |15 |21 |1070 |15 |21 |1160 |15 Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]] --> {{Documentation subpage}} {{Use template sandbox testcases request}} {{Lua|Module:Climate chart}}
This template can be used to create climate charts, with average monthly minimum and maximum temperatures.
=== How to read a climate chart ===
{{Climate chart/How to read a climate chart}}
=== Usage ===
;argument <kbd>1</kbd> :Appears in the heading ;arguments <kbd>2</kbd>–<kbd>37</kbd> :The data. Think of it as 12 rows, one for each month, with left column for average low temperature, middle column for average high temperature and right column for precipitation. ;<kbd>maxprecip=</kbd> :Supply highest monthly precipitation number if the blue precipitation bars pop out of the chart (happens if any of the monthly numbers is greater than about 750 mm (30 in.)) ;<kbd>float=</kbd> :<kbd>''left''</kbd>, <kbd>''right''</kbd> or <kbd>''none''</kbd>. ;<kbd>clear=</kbd> :<kbd>''left''</kbd>, <kbd>''right''</kbd>, <kbd>''both''</kbd> or <kbd>''none''</kbd>. ;<kbd>source=</kbd> :Supply the source of your data
====Empty syntax==== <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{climate chart | <!--- India ---> | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |maxprecip = <!--- supply largest monthly precipitation, in case it's > 750 mm (30 in.) ---> |float = <!--- left, right, or none ---> |clear = <!--- left, right, both, or none ---> |units = <!--- set to "imperial" if the values are in °F and inches ---> |source = <!--- source of the data ---> }} </syntaxhighlight>
=== Units === In articles where SI units (°C, mm) are preferred, input the data in those units. This is the default state — no further parameters are needed. In articles where imperial units (°F, inches) are preferred, input data in those units and specify the parameter units=imperial (see below). ''This parameter must be used so that the units are labeled correctly and so the conversion is done properly.''
=== Examples === ==== Float: right ====
{{climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=right }}
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" highlight="15,16" style="white-space: pre-wrap;"> {{climate chart | [[Amsterdam]] |0.5|5.4|62.1 |0.2|6.0|43.4 |2.4|9.2|58.9 |4.0|12.4|41.0 |7.8|17.1|48.3 |10.4|19.2|67.5 |12.5|21.4|65.8 |12.3|21.8|61.4 |10.2|18.4|82.1 |7.0|14.1|85.1 |3.9|9.2|89.0 |1.9|6.2|74.9 |float=right |clear=right }} </syntaxhighlight> {{clear}}
==== Float: left ====
{{climate chart | [[Bilbao]] |6|13|15 |6|14|15 |7|16|15 |8|17|17 |11|19|16 |14|22|12 |16|25|11 |16|25|11 |14|24|11 |12|21|15 |9|17|15 |7|14|14 |float=left |source=[http://www.bbc.co.uk/weather/ BBC Weather] }}
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" highlight="15" style="white-space: pre-wrap;"> {{climate chart | [[Bilbao]] |6|13|15 |6|14|15 |7|16|15 |8|17|17 |11|19|16 |14|22|12 |16|25|11 |16|25|11 |14|24|11 |12|21|15 |9|17|15 |7|14|14 |float=left |source=[http://www.bbc.co.uk/weather/ BBC Weather] }} </syntaxhighlight> {{clear}}

<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" style="white-space: pre-wrap;"> {{climate chart | [[Coober Pedy, South Australia|Coober Pedy]] |20.7|36.4|17.4 |20.8|35.7|22.8 |18.2|32.8|12.9 |14|27.6|6.5 |10.1|23.3|13.1 |7.2|18.8|14.4 |6.3|18.7|7.8 |7.4|20.7|9.2 |10.1|24.5|8.4 |13.6|28.9|14.9 |16.6|32.1|11.6 |19.1|34.6|17.5 }} </syntaxhighlight>
==== Imperial units ====
{{climate chart | [[Anchorage, Alaska|Anchorage]] |9|22|.68 |12|26|.74 |18|34|.65 |29|44|.52 |39|55|.7 |47|62|1.06 |52|65|1.7 |49|63|2.93 |41|55|2.87 |28|40|2.09 |16|28|1.09 |11|24|1.075 |float=right |clear=right |units=imperial }}
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" highlight="17" style="white-space: pre-wrap;"> {{climate chart | [[Anchorage, Alaska|Anchorage]] |9|22|.68 |12|26|.74 |18|34|.65 |29|44|.52 |39|55|.7 |47|62|1.06 |52|65|1.7 |49|63|2.93 |41|55|2.87 |28|40|2.09 |16|28|1.09 |11|24|1.05 |float=right |clear=right |units=imperial }} </syntaxhighlight>
==== Large precipitation numbers (without re-scaling) ====
{{climate chart |[[Baguio City, Philippines]] |12 |22 |20 |13 |22 |20 |14 |24 |40 |15 |25 |100 |16 |24 |400 |16 |23 |430 |15 |21 |1070 |15 |21 |1160 |15 |21 |710 |15 |22 |380 |15 |23 |120 |13 |23 |50 |float=right |clear=right }}
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" highlight="" style="white-space: pre-wrap;"> {{climate chart |[[Baguio City, Philippines]] |12 |22 |20 |13 |22 |20 |14 |24 |40 |15 |25 |100 |16 |24 |400 |16 |23 |430 |15 |21 |1070 |15 |21 |1160 |15 |21 |710 |15 |22 |380 |15 |23 |120 |13 |23 |50 |float=right |clear=right }} </syntaxhighlight>
==== Large precipitation numbers (with re-scaling) ====
{{climate chart |[[Baguio City, Philippines]] |12 |22 |20 |13 |22 |20 |14 |24 |40 |15 |25 |100 |16 |24 |400 |16 |23 |430 |15 |21 |1070 |15 |21 |1160 |15 |21 |710 |15 |22 |380 |15 |23 |120 |13 |23 |50 |maxprecip=1160 |float=right |clear=right }}
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" highlight="10,15" style="white-space: pre-wrap;"> {{climate chart |[[Baguio City, Philippines]] |12 |22 |20 |13 |22 |20 |14 |24 |40 |15 |25 |100 |16 |24 |400 |16 |23 |430 |15 |21 |1070 |15 |21 |1160 |15 |21 |710 |15 |22 |380 |15 |23 |120 |13 |23 |50 |maxprecip=1160 |float=right |clear=right }} </syntaxhighlight> Values of {{para|maxprecip}} less than or equal to 750 have no effect.
